title: Review: secretx full-project code review
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The project has very solid documentation and error handling. Main risks are: (1) gcp-sm put()+get() with pinned version silently violates contract, (2) several backends silently ignore ?field= in get() creating a footgun for users who expect field extraction everywhere, (3) GCP access token expiry in long-running processes will generate production incidents for daemon users.
Confidence: 85% chance of no missed bugs. Uncertainty driven by: (1) the hand-rolled JSON scanner (json_extract_string_field, json_navigate, json_find_value_b) is complex enough that edge cases in surrogate-pair handling or Unicode multi-byte sequences could have bugs not caught by existing tests; (2) PKCS#11 session management has platform-specific behavior I cannot test without hardware; (3) integration-untested backends (azure-kv, gcp-sm, doppler, bitwarden) have plausible API shape mismatches I cannot verify. Would raise confidence if: full JSON scanner fuzzing against a reference serde_json implementation; integration tests for all backends; explicit test for json_find_value_b with multi-byte UTF-8 keys and surrogate-pair-containing values.
